title: lessingianthus erythrophilus
intro: Lessingianthus erythrophilus, also known as Brazilian button, is a flowering plant native to Brazil and other parts of South America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and small, button-like shape. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lessingianthus erythrophilus include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or using them in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
